Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets recall the typical sequence for a Hire to Retire process 1 Post Job Ad 2 Screen Candidates 3 Conduct Interviews 4 Make Hiring Decision 5 Onboard Employee 6 Add to Payroll 7 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This means Decide can happen immediately after Screen or Interview can happen after Screen but theres no requirement that Decide happens after Interview

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Actually in a partial order if theres no path from A to B or B to A they can be executed in any order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So you could Screen Interview Decide correct Screen Decide Interview illogical deciding before interviewing or Screen Interview and Screen Decide in parallel also illogical

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a significant anomaly because it allows making hiring decisions before or without conducting interviews

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interview and Onboard could be in any order relative to each other This is problematic because you should interview before making a decision and onboarding should only happen after a positive decision which should come after interviews

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Model 2 structure loop onboarding means Onboard can be executed one or more times xor payroll is an exclusive choice between Payroll and skip do nothing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Post Interview This means you can go directly from posting the job ad to conducting interviews without screening candidates

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Since theres no order between Screen and Interview they could happen in parallel or in any order

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is problematic because screening should precede interviewing

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: The loop on onboarding might imply that onboarding can be repeated which might not make sense for a single employee

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: xor payroll means that after onboarding you either add to payroll or skip it Skipping payroll would be abnormal typically every onboarded employee should be added to payroll

Name: Validation - PE
Text: There is no edge from Screen to Decide or Screen to anything else except that its a node This means that Screen can happen but its not connected to the rest of the process

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So in Model 2 Screening is completely optional and disconnected from the rest of the process You could hire someone without screening anyone

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In Model 1 the minimal path includes Screen but not Interview In Model 2 the minimal path includes Interview but not Screen

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Missing Interview is probably worse than missing Screen because interviews are a more substantial evaluation step

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: However in Model 2 you also have the issue that Payroll can be skipped which is a very serious problem because the employee wouldnt get paid

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Model 1 all activities are mandatory no choices while in Model 2 Payroll may not be executed in some traces

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 is closer to the normative process because all activities are mandatory and most dependencies are correct except for the order between Interview Decide and Onboard

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 has optional Screening can be skipped entirely or done out of order optional Payroll can be skipped and some ordering issues

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The mandatory nature of all activities in Model 1 is a big plus over Model 2s optional Payroll

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Model 1 all nodes must be executed in every trace In Model 2 Payroll may not be executed and Screening may not happen

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore Model 1 more closely aligns with a normative Hire to Retire process because it ensures all necessary activities are performed even if some may occur in a suboptimal order
